fields.label: Apply to all doctypes that link to this DocType
fields.rationale: Default 1 is the usual intent: 'restrict the user to records where <allow> = <for_value>' cascades to every DocType that carries a Link to <allow>. When 0, the restriction applies ONLY to direct access of <allow> records and does NOT propagate to dependent DocTypes - useful for niche hybrid setups where the user should see e.g. their own Customer but all Sales Invoices regardless of customer.

fields.label: Applicable for DocType (when apply_to_all_doctypes=0)
fields.rationale: Only meaningful when apply_to_all_doctypes=0. Names a specific DocType where this permission row applies. Multiple rows per user (same allow / for_value, different applicable_for) author the 'user can see this record only in this doctype' shape.

fields.label: Hide descendants (for tree DocTypes)
fields.rationale: Only meaningful when the <allow> DocType is a tree (is_tree=1). When 1, child records of the permitted node are hidden as well - useful for hierarchical orgs where a user should see ONLY their territory and not its sub-territories. Default 0 (descendants visible).

fields.label: Default value for the user
fields.rationale: When 1, this permission's for_value auto-populates the corresponding Link field on new documents the user creates (e.g. default Company on a new Sales Invoice). Only ONE User Permission row per (user, allow) pair can have is_default=1.
